backend.architecture_style: REST API / Monolith with modular service layer

services.name: OrderService
services.responsibility: Retrieves, filters, and manages order records. Enforces the 30-day window query and status filtering logic.

services.name: OrderRepository
services.responsibility: Direct database access layer for order data. Executes parameterised queries against the orders table.

services.name: PaymentMaskingService
services.responsibility: Applies masking rules to payment reference numbers before any data leaves the backend. Masking direction and character are pending confirmation (see open questions).

services.name: NotificationService
services.responsibility: Sends email notifications to customers on order status changes. Handles retry logic and failure logging. Triggers and template content are pending confirmation.

services.name: EmailAdapter
services.responsibility: Abstracts SMTP/email provider integration. Accepts a structured message object and dispatches it. Provider selection is deferred pending infrastructure decision.

services.name: AuthService
services.responsibility: Validates JWT tokens and enforces role-based access control. Role definitions (e.g. 'operations_staff') are pending clarification.

services.name: UserRepository
services.responsibility: Reads user and role data from the database to support authentication and authorisation checks.

services.name: AuditService
services.responsibility: Records audit log entries for order data access and status change events. Included as a placeholder pending confirmation of audit logging requirements.

services.name: AuditRepository
services.responsibility: Persists audit log records to the audit_log table.

api_endpoints.description: Returns orders within the last 30 days (rolling window assumed — see open questions). Supports optional status filter query parameter(s). Payment reference numbers are masked in the response.
api_endpoints.request_body: None. Query params: status (string or array — multi-select pending confirmation), page (integer), page_size (integer).
api_endpoints.response: Paginated list of order objects with masked payment references. Fields: order_id, customer_id, status, created_at, updated_at, masked_payment_reference.

api_endpoints.description: Returns the full detail of a single order. Payment reference is masked. Accessible to authorised users only.
api_endpoints.response: Single order object with all fields and masked payment reference.

api_endpoints.description: Updates the status of an order. Triggers the NotificationService to send an email to the customer if applicable. Exact trigger conditions are pending clarification.
api_endpoints.request_body: JSON: { new_status: string } — must be one of: Pending, Processing, Shipped, Delivered, Cancelled.
api_endpoints.response: Updated order object with masked payment reference and status change timestamp.

api_endpoints.description: Returns the list of valid order status values. Allows frontend to populate filter dropdowns dynamically.
api_endpoints.response: Array of status strings: [Pending, Processing, Shipped, Delivered, Cancelled].

api_endpoints.description: Authenticates a user and returns a signed JWT token for session management.
api_endpoints.response: JSON: { token: string, expires_at: string, user: { id, role } }

api_endpoints.description: Invalidates the current user session token.
api_endpoints.request_body: None. Requires valid Bearer token in Authorization header.

tables.name: users
tables.purpose: Stores system user accounts including operations staff. Role assignment links to the roles table.

columns.name: is_active
columns.description: Whether the user account is active

tables.name: roles
tables.purpose: Defines system roles for RBAC. Role names and permissions are placeholders pending clarification of RBAC requirements.

columns.name: description
columns.description: Description of the role and its permissions

tables.name: customers
tables.purpose: Stores customer records. Email is used for order notification dispatch.

columns.name: email
columns.description: Customer's registered email address for notifications

columns.name: notification_opt_out
columns.description: Whether the customer has opted out of email notifications. Included as a placeholder pending GDPR/opt-out confirmation.

tables.name: orders
tables.purpose: Core order records. Payment reference is stored in full and masked only at the application layer on output.

columns.name: customer_id
columns.description: The customer who placed the order

columns.name: status
columns.description: Current order status. Enum: Pending, Processing, Shipped, Delivered, Cancelled

columns.name: payment_reference
columns.description: Full unmasked payment reference. Masked at application layer before any output. Storage encryption recommended.

columns.name: placed_at
columns.description: Timestamp when the order was placed. Used for 30-day window query.

tables.name: order_status_history
tables.purpose: Immutable log of all order status changes. Supports audit trail and notification triggering.

columns.name: history_id
columns.description: Unique identifier for this history entry

columns.name: order_id
columns.description: The order this status change belongs to

columns.name: previous_status
columns.description: Status before the change. Null for initial creation.

columns.name: changed_by
columns.description: The user who made the status change

tables.name: notification_log
tables.purpose: Records all email notification attempts, outcomes, and retry state for order status change emails.

columns.name: notification_id
columns.description: Unique identifier for the notification record

columns.name: order_id
columns.description: The order this notification relates to

columns.name: trigger_status
columns.description: The order status that triggered this notification

columns.name: sent_at
columns.description: Timestamp of successful dispatch. Null if not yet sent.

columns.name: status
columns.description: Notification status: PENDING, SENT, FAILED, RETRY

tables.name: audit_log
tables.purpose: Append-only audit trail of significant system events (data access, status changes). Included as a design placeholder pending confirmation of audit requirements.

columns.name: audit_id
columns.description: Unique identifier for the audit entry

columns.name: resource_type
columns.description: The entity type acted upon (e.g. order, notification)

columns.name: resource_id
columns.description: The specific entity ID acted upon

columns.name: metadata
columns.description: Additional structured context for the event

relationships.description: Each user is assigned one role; a role may be held by many users
Each order belongs to one customer; a customer may have many orders
Each status history entry belongs to one order; an order may have many history entries
Each status change is attributed to one user
Each notification is associated with one order; an order may have many notifications
Each notification is associated with one customer
Each audit entry is associated with the acting user

database.erd_mermaid:
erDiagram
  ROLES {
    UUID role_id PK
    VARCHAR role_name
    TEXT description
  }
  USERS {
    UUID user_id PK
    VARCHAR username
    VARCHAR email
    VARCHAR password_hash
    UUID role_id FK
    BOOLEAN is_active
    TIMESTAMPTZ created_at
    TIMESTAMPTZ updated_at
  }
  CUSTOMERS {
    UUID customer_id PK
    VARCHAR full_name
    VARCHAR email
    BOOLEAN notification_opt_out
    TIMESTAMPTZ created_at
    TIMESTAMPTZ updated_at
  }
  ORDERS {
    UUID order_id PK
    UUID customer_id FK
    order_status_enum status
    VARCHAR payment_reference
    TIMESTAMPTZ placed_at
    TIMESTAMPTZ created_at
    TIMESTAMPTZ updated_at
  }
  ORDER_STATUS_HISTORY {
    UUID history_id PK
    UUID order_id FK
    order_status_enum previous_status
    order_status_enum new_status
    UUID changed_by FK
    TIMESTAMPTZ changed_at
  }
  NOTIFICATION_LOG {
    UUID notification_id PK
    UUID order_id FK
    UUID customer_id FK
    order_status_enum trigger_status
    TIMESTAMPTZ sent_at
    VARCHAR status
    INTEGER retry_count
    TEXT failure_reason
  }
  AUDIT_LOG {
    UUID audit_id PK
    UUID actor_user_id FK
    VARCHAR action_type
    VARCHAR resource_type
    UUID resource_id
    TIMESTAMPTZ occurred_at
    JSONB metadata
  }
  ROLES ||--o{ USERS : "assigned to"
  CUSTOMERS ||--o{ ORDERS : "places"
  ORDERS ||--o{ ORDER_STATUS_HISTORY : "has"
  USERS ||--o{ ORDER_STATUS_HISTORY : "records"
  ORDERS ||--o{ NOTIFICATION_LOG : "triggers"
  CUSTOMERS ||--o{ NOTIFICATION_LOG : "receives"
  USERS ||--o{ AUDIT_LOG : "performs"

components.name: App
components.purpose: Root component. Manages routing and global auth context.

components.name: AuthGuard
components.purpose: HOC / wrapper that redirects unauthenticated users to the login screen.

components.name: LoginPage
components.purpose: Renders the login form and handles authentication submission.

components.name: LoginForm
components.purpose: Controlled form component for username and password input.

components.name: MainLayout
components.purpose: Persistent layout shell containing the header and navigation.

components.name: Header
components.purpose: Displays application name, current user, and logout action.

components.name: OrderListPage
components.purpose: Primary view. Fetches and displays the 30-day order list with filter controls.

components.name: OrderFilterBar
components.purpose: Renders status filter controls. Emits filter state changes to OrderListPage.

components.name: OrderTable
components.purpose: Renders paginated order rows. Displays masked payment references.

components.name: OrderTableRow
components.purpose: Renders a single order row. Displays order_id, customer, status, placed_at, masked_payment_reference.

components.name: StatusBadge
components.purpose: Visual pill/badge component for displaying order status with colour coding.

components.name: Pagination
components.purpose: Controls for navigating between pages of order results.

components.name: OrderDetailPage
components.purpose: Full detail view for a single order including status history.

components.name: OrderDetailCard
components.purpose: Displays all fields of a single order with masked payment reference.

components.name: StatusHistoryTimeline
components.purpose: Renders the chronological list of status changes for an order.

components.name: UpdateStatusModal
components.purpose: Modal dialog for operations staff to change an order status.

components.name: NotificationBanner
components.purpose: Inline alert component for success, error, and informational feedback.

components.name: LoadingSpinner
components.purpose: Reusable loading indicator shown during async data fetching.

user_flows.name: Login Flow
steps: User navigates to the application URL
AuthGuard detects no valid session and redirects to LoginPage
User enters username and password in LoginForm
Frontend POSTs credentials to /api/auth/login
On success, JWT is stored in memory / secure cookie and user is redirected to OrderListPage
On failure, error message is displayed in LoginForm
user_flows.flow_mermaid:
flowchart TD
  A[User visits app] --> B{Valid session?}
  B -- No --> C[LoginPage]
  C --> D[Enter credentials]
  D --> E[POST /api/auth/login]
  E --> F{Auth success?}
  F -- Yes --> G[Store JWT, redirect to OrderListPage]
  F -- No --> H[Show error in LoginForm]
  H --> D
  B -- Yes --> G

user_flows.name: View and Filter Orders Flow
steps: Authenticated user lands on OrderListPage
Frontend automatically calls GET /api/orders (default: last 30 days, no status filter)
OrderTable renders paginated results with masked payment references
User selects one or more status filters in OrderFilterBar
Frontend calls GET /api/orders with status query parameter(s)
OrderTable re-renders with filtered results
User clicks a row to navigate to OrderDetailPage
user_flows.flow_mermaid:
flowchart TD
  A[OrderListPage loads] --> B[GET /api/orders]
  B --> C[Render OrderTable with results]
  C --> D{User applies status filter?}
  D -- Yes --> E[GET /api/orders?status=...]
  E --> C
  D -- No --> F{User clicks order row?}
  C --> F
  F -- Yes --> G[Navigate to OrderDetailPage]
  F -- No --> C

user_flows.name: Update Order Status Flow
steps: User is viewing OrderDetailPage for a specific order
User clicks 'Update Status' button
UpdateStatusModal opens with current status displayed
User selects new status from dropdown
User confirms the change
Frontend sends PATCH /api/orders/:orderId/status
Backend updates status, writes to order_status_history, triggers NotificationService
On success, OrderDetailCard refreshes with new status and NotificationBanner shows confirmation
On failure, NotificationBanner shows error message
user_flows.flow_mermaid:
flowchart TD
  A[OrderDetailPage] --> B[Click Update Status]
  B --> C[UpdateStatusModal opens]
  C --> D[Select new status]
  D --> E[Confirm]
  E --> F[PATCH /api/orders/:id/status]
  F --> G{API success?}
  G -- Yes --> H[Refresh OrderDetailCard]
  H --> I[Show success NotificationBanner]
  G -- No --> J[Show error NotificationBanner]
  J --> C

wireframes.description: Minimal authentication screen for operations staff to log in.
Main operations view showing orders from the last 30 days with status filtering and pagination.
Full order record view with status history timeline and option to update status.
Modal overlay for changing an order's status from the Order Detail Page.

flowchart TD

diagrams.component_hierarchy_mermaid:
flowchart TD
  App --> AuthGuard
  App --> LoginPage
  App --> NotificationBanner
  App --> LoadingSpinner
  App --> MainLayout
  LoginPage --> LoginForm
  MainLayout --> Header
  MainLayout --> OrderListPage
  MainLayout --> OrderDetailPage

  OrderTableRow --> StatusBadge

  OrderDetailPage --> UpdateStatusModal

design_decisions.decision: REQ-003 (Prompt Injection attempt) is entirely excluded from all design artefacts.
design_decisions.rationale: The Requirements Agent correctly identified this entry as an adversarial prompt injection, not a business requirement. It has been treated as data only and no instructions within it have been followed, consistent with system prompt directives.
alternatives_considered: Include as a flagged non-functional requirement for security documentation — rejected as it has no design expression

design_decisions.decision: PostgreSQL selected as the database engine.
design_decisions.rationale: The requirements describe relational data with clear foreign key relationships (users, orders, customers, audit). A relational engine with strong ACID guarantees is appropriate for a financial services order management context.
alternatives_considered: MongoDB — rejected due to relational nature of data and financial audit requirements
MySQL — viable alternative, PostgreSQL preferred for JSONB audit metadata support and maturity

design_decisions.decision: Monolithic REST API with a modular service layer, rather than microservices.
design_decisions.rationale: The current requirements scope covers a single bounded domain (order management) with a limited set of services. Microservices would introduce unnecessary operational complexity at this stage. The modular service layer allows future extraction if needed.
alternatives_considered: GraphQL — adds flexibility but increases complexity without clear requirement for it

design_decisions.decision: Payment reference numbers are stored in full in the database and masked exclusively at the application layer before any API response or UI render.
design_decisions.rationale: Masking at the application layer ensures a single, consistent enforcement point across all output surfaces (API, UI, notifications). The database retains the full value for operational and audit use cases. Column-level encryption at rest is noted as a recommendation pending security review.
alternatives_considered: Store only the masked value — rejected as it removes operational recovery options
Mask in the database view layer — fragile and harder to audit

design_decisions.decision: notification_opt_out column included in the customers table as a placeholder field.
design_decisions.rationale: REQ-004 is incomplete regarding customer opt-out and GDPR compliance. Rather than blocking the database schema, a boolean opt_out flag is included as a minimal placeholder. Its business logic is not wired until requirements are confirmed.
alternatives_considered: Omit entirely until requirements are confirmed — rejected as retrofitting the schema later is higher risk
Separate notification_preferences table — deferred until opt-out scope is clarified

design_decisions.decision: audit_log table included as a placeholder pending confirmation of audit requirements.
design_decisions.rationale: The overarching clarifying questions in the requirements document explicitly flag audit logging as potentially required for regulatory compliance in a financial services context. Designing it out and retrofitting later would be high risk. It is included but not fully specified.
alternatives_considered: Omit and add later — high risk in FS regulatory context
Third-party audit logging service — deferred pending NFR clarification

design_decisions.decision: 30-day order window is designed as a rolling window from the current timestamp.
design_decisions.rationale: This is the most common interpretation and the most operationally useful (always shows the most recent 30 days). However, this is explicitly flagged as an open question and the implementation should be treated as provisional.
alternatives_considered: Fixed calendar month — possible alternative if confirmed by BA

design_decisions.decision: Status filter designed to support multi-select (array of status values) at the API layer.
design_decisions.rationale: Multi-select is the more capable design and is no more complex to implement than single-select. It is also the more likely business requirement. Confirmed as an open question — single-select can be enforced by the frontend without changing the API contract if required.
alternatives_considered: Single-select only — restrictive and likely to require rework

open_questions.question: REQ-001: Is the 30-day order window a rolling window relative to the current date/time, or fixed to the current calendar month?
open_questions.impact: The query logic in OrderService and the label shown on the OrderListPage header depend on this. The design currently assumes a rolling window.

open_questions.question: REQ-001: Should cancelled orders be included in the default 30-day order view?
open_questions.impact: Determines whether the default GET /api/orders query filters out Cancelled status. If excluded by default, the filter bar behaviour must also account for this.

open_questions.question: REQ-001 / REQ-002: What is the defined 'operations staff' role — is it a specific named RBAC role, and what permissions does it carry compared to other roles (e.g. admin, read-only)?
open_questions.impact: The roles table, AuthService, and all auth_required API endpoints cannot be fully specified until the RBAC model is confirmed. Role names in the current design are placeholders.
open_questions.raised_by: requirements gap / ambiguity — REQ-001, overall clarifying questions

open_questions.question: REQ-002: Can users filter by multiple statuses simultaneously (multi-select), or is only single-status filtering required?
open_questions.impact: Affects the OrderFilterBar component UX design (checkboxes vs radio buttons) and the API query parameter contract for GET /api/orders.

open_questions.question: REQ-002: Does the status filter apply only within the 30-day window view, or can users filter across all orders regardless of date range?
open_questions.impact: If filtering must work across all orders, the GET /api/orders endpoint needs an optional date_range parameter and the OrderListPage requires a date range control. This significantly expands scope.

open_questions.question: REQ-002: Is the status list (Pending, Processing, Shipped, Delivered, Cancelled) fixed and exhaustive, or may additional statuses be added in future?
open_questions.impact: Determines whether status is implemented as a database enum (rigid) or a reference/lookup table (extensible). Changing from enum to lookup table after go-live is a breaking schema change.

open_questions.question: REQ-004: Which status transitions trigger an email notification — all transitions, or only specific ones (e.g. only Shipped and Delivered)?
open_questions.impact: The NotificationService trigger logic, notification_log records, and email template count all depend on this. Cannot finalise NotificationService design without this answer.

open_questions.question: REQ-004: What is the required behaviour when email delivery fails — how many retries, over what interval, and what alerting or escalation is expected?
open_questions.impact: The retry_count and status fields in notification_log are placeholders. The NotificationService retry strategy cannot be designed without this. This is a testable acceptance criterion gap.

open_questions.question: REQ-004: Must customers be able to opt out of email notifications, and if so, how is that preference managed (self-service UI, admin override, etc.)?
open_questions.impact: The notification_opt_out column in the customers table is a placeholder. If opt-out is required, a customer preferences screen and associated API endpoint must be added. There may also be GDPR implications.

open_questions.question: REQ-004: Are there regulatory or compliance requirements (e.g. GDPR, FCA) governing the use of customer email addresses for notifications in this financial services context?
open_questions.impact: Could require consent tracking, data processing records, and a customer-facing preference management feature. Potentially a significant scope addition.

open_questions.question: REQ-005: Please confirm the intended masking direction — should the LAST 4 digits be masked (showing the prefix), or should all digits EXCEPT the last 4 be masked (showing only the suffix, e.g. ****1234)?
open_questions.impact: These are opposite security behaviours. The PaymentMaskingService cannot be specified until this is confirmed. All wireframes showing masked payment references are provisional. A mis-implementation here is a security defect.

open_questions.question: REQ-005: Does payment reference masking apply to all output surfaces — API responses, email notifications, data exports, and printed reports — or only to on-screen UI display?
open_questions.impact: If masking applies to email notifications, the NotificationService email template must apply masking. If it applies to API responses, all API endpoints returning order data must pass through PaymentMaskingService. The current design assumes all surfaces but this must be confirmed.

open_questions.question: REQ-005: Should any user role (e.g. admin) ever be permitted to view an unmasked payment reference number, and under what conditions?
open_questions.impact: If elevated privilege unmasking is required, a separate privileged endpoint with additional audit logging must be designed. This is currently not in scope.

open_questions.question: Overall: Are there non-functional requirements for this system — response time SLAs, availability targets, maximum concurrent users, or data retention policies for order records?
open_questions.impact: Without NFRs, no performance optimisations (e.g. database indexing strategy, caching layer, CDN) can be justified in the design. Pagination is included defensively but page size limits cannot be set without volume expectations.

open_questions.question: Overall: Are there audit logging requirements for order data access or status changes for regulatory compliance?
open_questions.impact: The audit_log table is included as a placeholder. If audit logging is not required, it can be removed. If it is required, the retention period, query interface, and export capability must be specified.

pipeline_metadata.handoff_blocked_reason: Multiple open questions from REQ-004 and REQ-005 prevent the QA Agent from writing complete test cases: the email notification trigger conditions, failure handling behaviour, and payment reference masking direction are all unconfirmed, making acceptance criteria for these requirements untestable.
